message:
feat(health,sync): degraded health state for WAL corruption detection and major source sync expansion

Health check hardening:
- Detect and report "degraded" state when the database file exists but
  cannot be opened (e.g. WAL frame salt mismatch, corruption) — previously
  this was incorrectly reported as "healthy" since only file existence
  was checked
- Capture DB open errors in state_meta_json with WAL-specific error
  classification (salt mismatch, corruption keywords)
- Expand --json health output with status field ("healthy"/"degraded"/
  "unhealthy"), errors array, and structured db section (exists, opened,
  conversations, messages, open_error)
- Add degraded terminal output with recovery guidance ("cass doctor --fix"
  or "cass index --full")
- Return exit code 1 for degraded state with descriptive error message
- Document invariant: --json mode ALWAYS emits valid JSON even on
  corrupt databases

Source sync expansion:
- Major expansion of source sync module (~481 lines) with improved
  reconciliation logic, additional source type handling, and enhanced
  error propagation paths

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
